one_time_description: Gain from Government Business Partnerships (primarily Petroglyph Development Group LP) of $14,754,820 compared to prior year loss of $1,471,607 - large non-recurring swing in GBP equity earnings
economic_notes: Nation holds 99% of Petroglyph Development Group Limited Partnership and 49.90% of Salpac Fisheries Limited Partnership accounted for under modified equity method. Also holds 100% of Tuytaxun General Store Ltd. and 100% of Petroglyph Development Group Ltd. Active in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP. Multiple specific land claims negotiations ongoing (Douglas Treaty Village Claims). FNFA loans partially on-lent to PDG LP. Thlap'qwum Community Settlement Trust distributions received.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on three bases: (1) Petroglyph Development Group LP uses ASPE instead of IFRS as required by PSAS after Dec 31 2022 - impact on GBP investment and surplus unknown; (2) Tuytaxun General Store Ltd (GBE) and Salpac Fisheries LP (GBP) were not audited and Salpac financial statements for FY2022/2023/2024 not available; (3) condensed supplementary financial information for GBEs and GBPs not disclosed publicly by Chief and Council decision. Prior period restatement of $750,000 applied to 2023 comparative figures - Province of BC funding reclassified to deferred revenue. Consolidated entities include La'Lum'utul Housing Society, Snuneymuxw Youth and Family Society, and 1216476 B.C. Ltd. Housing expenses split between Housing ($1,679,723) and Transitional Housing ($96,555) totalling $1,776,278. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + FNHA + BC Housing + BC Treaty Commission + City of Nanaimo + CSEA&TS + FNESS + DFO + Province of BC = $13,571,432 + $952,586 + $2,620,998 + $4,759,427 + $411,997 + $217,265 + $417,021 + $474,418 + $1,379,052 + $2,158,002 + $509,902 (United Way) = approximately $27,471,100; OSR includes interest, rental, miscellaneous, gaming revenue sharing, trust distributions, GBP/GBE gains/losses. Restricted cash of $13,512,856 includes CMHC reserves ($954,125) and internally restricted capital project funds ($12,558,731).
